# Let's open the file and print its content and length and show repr
path = "/mnt/data/file-QodrnLthRuUr9rRdNAUfP2-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "rb") as f:
    data = f.read()
data[:1000], len(data)

s = open("/mnt/data/file-QodrnLthRuUr9rRdNAUfP2-text_to_analyse.txt","r", encoding="utf-8").read()

spans = [
"وَمَن يَكُن حَلِيمًا وَمَن يَسْرِف فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌ عَنْهُ",
"لا بُخلَ فيما يُعتَدُّ بِهِ",
"وَلَوْ أَنَّ قُرَيْشًا يُفْتَنُونَ فِي الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ مِن شَرِّ الدَّجَالِ وَرِقَامٍ وَنِفَاقٍ وَجَمعٍ",
"لا تُبَخِلِ المَرءَ في حَياتِهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ يُبَخِلُ مَن يُبَخِلُ وَيُعطي مَن يُعطي"
]

# Find all occurrences of each span
occurrences = []
for span in spans:
    start = 0
    while True:
        idx = s.find(span, start)
        if idx == -1:
            break
        occurrences.append((span, idx, idx+len(span)))
        start = idx+1  # continue search after this index to find overlapping occurrences if any

occurrences

def classify(span, start, end):
    context = s[max(0,start-64):min(len(s),end+64)]
    return context

for span, start, end in occurrences:
    print("SPAN:",repr(span))
    print("start,end:", start, end)
    ctx = classify(span,start,end)
    print("Context snippet:", ctx.replace("\n","\\n"))
    print("---")

import json
entries = []
for span, start, end in sorted(occurrences, key=lambda x: x[1]):
    text = s[start:end]
    # classify using cues
    ctx = s[max(0,start-64):min(len(s),end+64)]
    ayah_cues = ["قال الله تعالى","يقول الله تعالى","في القرآن الكريم","قال الله","يقول الله","القرآن الكريم","سورة","الآية","البقرة:","النمل:"]
    hadith_cues = ["قال الرسول","قال رسول الله","النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم","رواه","رواية","رواه البخاري","رواه مسلم","في الحديث","روى"]
    ctype = None
    lowctx = ctx
    if any(c in lowctx for c in ayah_cues) and not any(c in lowctx for c in hadith_cues):
        ctype = "Aya"
    elif any(c in lowctx for c in hadith_cues) and not any(c in lowctx for c in ayah_cues):
        ctype = "Hadeeth"
    else:
        # fallback: if span contains words like اللَّهَ or Quran ??? But here default by span: if span contains Allah name maybe ayah? But better inspect mapping known occurrences.
        # For safety, map known spans by content
        if "يَكُن" in text or "قُرَيْشًا" in text or "وَلَوْ" in text:
            ctype = "Aya"
        else:
            ctype = "Hadeeth"
    entries.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": text,
        "citation_type": ctype
    })

print(json.dumps({"citations": entries}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))
